title: A Bird is an Egg with Wings
description: Forget the details. A bird, in its simplest form, is an egg. In this lesson you'll learn to see the basic structure: oval for the body, circle for the head, and guide lines for movement. Draw 5 birds in different positions using only these shapes.

title: Beaks, Feet and Eyes
description: Now that you have the base shape, it's time to give identity. Beaks change according to the bird's diet, feet according to its habitat, and eyes according to its size. I'll show you how to simplify these parts without losing recognizability. Practice adding these elements to your birds from the previous lesson.

title: Feathers: Texture Without Copying Every Feather
description: The most common mistake is wanting to draw feather by feather. Here I'll teach you to suggest texture with directional strokes, shadow groupings, and soft edges. You'll practice with smooth-plumaged birds (sparrows) and birds with showy plumage (woodpeckers, bluebirds).

title: Birds in Action: Flying, Perched, Singing
description: Life isn't static. Learn to draw birds in motion: wings extended, heads turned, feet gripping branches. We'll use photo references but also direct observation. Your final exercise: a small sketchbook of 3 different birds in different postures.

title: The Head is a Sphere: Finding the Structure
description: Before exaggeration comes understanding. Learn to see the head as a sphere in space, find the center lines, and place the basic features. You'll practice drawing 5 different head angles from reference, focusing on proportion before distortion.

title: The Rules of Exaggeration
description: Exaggeration isn't random—it follows patterns. In this lesson you'll learn to identify distinctive features and amplify them while maintaining likeness. We'll analyze faces together and practice: big noses get bigger, small eyes get smaller, wide jaws get wider.

title: Expression: The Soul of Caricature
description: A caricature without expression is just a distorted portrait. Learn to capture emotions by observing how features move: eyebrows that lift, mouths that stretch, cheeks that bunch. You'll practice drawing the same face with 5 different expressions.

title: Style: Making It Yours
description: Now that you know the rules, it's time to break them intentionally. This final lesson explores different caricature styles: from realistic to cartoonish, from subtle to extreme. Your project: create your own caricature style guide and apply it to 3 different faces.

title: Seeing Light: Values and Their Boundaries
description: Light is information. In this first lesson you'll learn to identify the 5 essential value zones: highlight, light, midtone, shadow, reflected light. Practice with simple geometric shapes (sphere, cube, cylinder) before moving to complex objects.

title: Core Shadow and Cast Shadow
description: Not all shadows are the same. Learn the crucial difference between form shadow (turning away from light) and cast shadow (blocked light). You'll practice controlling edges: soft for form shadows, hard for cast shadows near the object.

title: The Gradient: Smooth Transitions
description: The secret to realistic volume is seamless gradation. This lesson focuses on pencil control for smooth value transitions. Practice exercises for hand pressure control, then apply to drawing a simple still life with 3 objects.

title: Reflected Light and Highlights
description: The details that bring drawings to life. Learn to observe and render reflected light (subtle brightness in shadow areas) and highlights (direct light on shiny surfaces). Practice with metal, glass, and matte objects to see the difference.

title: Composition with Light
description: Now you control light, not just observe it. Learn to design lighting for your drawings: dramatic single source, soft diffused light, backlighting. Practice drawing the same object under 3 different lighting setups.

title: Putting It All Together: A Complete Drawing
description: Final project: create a fully rendered drawing applying everything learned. Choose a subject with multiple surfaces and textures. Step-by-step demonstration followed by your independent work. Includes feedback guidelines for self-critique.
